creator: will@physics.purdue.edu
text:
On Mon, Sep 03, 2001 at 01:35:57AM -0500, Mike Meyer (mwm@mired.org) wrote:
> Not when it's invoked via make update in /usr/src, which will run
> "make update" if /etc/make.conf is set up properly.

That mechanism is crap in my opinion.
In any case, an update in /usr/src should not be trying to update
/usr/ports.. that violates enclosement and separation principles.
So src/Makefile needs to be fixed, not ports/Makefile.

> > should be ${MAKE}...  and I already told you why INDEX is just
> > slightly out of date.  It'll be updated for the release...
> 
> In that case, it needs to be fixed in the index target. I was
> surprised a bit surprised to see that index used a bar "make", but
> figured the author knew what they were doing and did it their way.

``a bar "make"''????

Nothing needs to be fixed in the index target.. there is no
problem there.

-- 
wca
